purpose: Machine-optimized routing instructions that tell an AI which project knowledge documents to reference when specific subjects, tasks, keywords, modules, contracts, channels, files, or UI areas are encountered.

matching_policy.minimum_required_action: If any P0/P1 trigger matches, the AI must reference all required_documents for that route before answering, planning, modifying code, or validating work.
matching_policy.multi_match_policy: If multiple routes match, union required_documents, preserve each route authority_order, and resolve conflicts using global_conflict_resolution plus subject route authority_order.
matching_policy.coding_task_extension: For any code-writing, code-review, debugging, refactoring, or file-location task, also reference DOC_FILE_MODULE_MAPPING and DOC_SERVICES_AI_REFERENCE unless the task is explicitly outside the repository.
matching_policy.mt4_extension: For any MT4, MQL4, EA, broker, OrderSend, WebRequest, DDE, DLL, Strategy Tester, native data, terminal UI, or platform capability task, always reference DOC_MT4_AUTHORITATIVE_REF first.
matching_policy.calendar_extension: For any economic calendar, CAL8, CAL5, RCI, ForexFactory, anticipation, proximity, event state, debounce, calendar CSV, file_seq, or checksum task, always reference DOC_CAL_DES_RULES before implementation references.
matching_policy.ui_extension: For any operator-facing dashboard, GUI, screen, panel, control, REST API, WebSocket, safety state, admin-gated action, or UI-visible field task, always reference DOC_UI_CATALOG.

global_conflict_resolution.rule: Subject-specific canonical JSON or active governance baseline wins over narrative notes, historical Markdown, extracted PDF text, and implementation samples.
MT4 platform behavior, native feature availability, broker variability, and native data authority are governed by DOC_MT4_AUTHORITATIVE_REF.
Module identity, module symbols, module responsibilities, dependencies, and interfaces are governed by DOC_MODULE_CATALOG.

Process step IDs, step codes, handoff contracts, and dependency_step_ids are governed by DOC_PROCESS_STEP_CATALOG. Phase grouping and validation/failure contract IDs are governed by DOC_ALIGNED_PROCESS.
MT4-Python channel IDs, ports, protocols, directions, owning modules, and data contracts are governed by DOC_COMM_CHANNELS.
Operator UI products, screens, controls, REST/WebSocket contracts, disabled-state rules, safety levels, and acceptance criteria are governed by DOC_UI_CATALOG.
Calendar deterministic decisions, CAL8/CAL5/RCI identity, anti-patterns, and single-writer calendar authority are governed by DOC_CAL_DES_RULES.
Physical file-to-module mapping is governed by DOC_FILE_MODULE_MAPPING, but file mapping does not override canonical module/process catalogs.
Generated/session-derived service references and implementation sketches must be verified against current source before destructive changes.
Implementation samples, especially DOC_EA_EXECUTION_ENGINE_SAMPLE, are lowest authority and must not override canonical contracts or platform references.

must_reference_when: A task mentions any canonical module symbol or asks which module owns a function, contract, file, or process step.
A task changes architecture boundaries, scope_in/scope_out, dependencies, or quality gates.
DOC_MODULE_CATALOG.conflict_role: wins for canonical module identity and module-level responsibility unless a newer explicitly versioned module catalog supersedes it.

must_reference_when: The task asks what output from one module becomes input to the next.
The task mentions process step IDs, S-numbers, validation, failure handling, or dependency order.
DOC_PROCESS_STEP_CATALOG.conflict_role: wins for step IDs, step codes, basic handoff contracts, responsible component names, and direct dependency_step_ids.

must_reference_when: The task asks for the current aligned process, phase sequence, validation contract, or failure contract.
The task converts or validates YAML/JSON process representations.
DOC_ALIGNED_PROCESS.conflict_role: wins for phase grouping and explicit validation_contract/failure_contract identifiers in the aligned v2 process representation.

must_reference_when: The task asks for the big-picture module map, service homes, module groups, bridge protocol, EA systems, or shared libraries.
DOC_MODULE_MAP.conflict_role: secondary to machine-readable catalogs for exact IDs/contracts, but useful as the readable architectural map.

must_reference_when: The task asks what files implement a module, which service owns a file, or whether a file is a test.
A coding task needs the physical path for a module or service.
DOC_FILE_MODULE_MAPPING.conflict_role: wins only for observed physical file-to-module mapping in the current file list; does not override canonical module definitions.

must_reference_when: The task concerns the repository services folder, downloaded techspec files, local paths, or the service inventory discovered from the terminal session.
DOC_SERVICES_AI_REFERENCE.conflict_role: session-derived context only; verify against live repo when available before making destructive changes.

trigger_task_types: decide whether to build custom solution
must_reference_when: The task asks whether an existing internal service/tool can solve a problem or whether custom code is necessary.
The task includes cost or usage-limit concerns.
DOC_CAPABILITY_REGISTRY.conflict_role: wins for cost/use-case metadata in the generated capability registry; secondary to implementation docs for actual behavior.

must_reference_when: The task mentions any MT4/Python transport, channel, port, Redis topic, DDE, CSV, JSONL, socket, or bridge data contract.
DOC_COMM_CHANNELS.conflict_role: wins for canonical channel IDs, status, directions, port assignments, protocols, owning modules, and data contracts.

trigger_task_types: inventory MQL4 and Python bridge files
must_reference_when: The task asks for the exact source files tied to MT4/Python communication or a narrative explanation of the bridge.
DOC_MT4_PY_CHANNELS_TXT.conflict_role: secondary to communication_channels.json for canonical status; useful for file discovery and explanatory context.

trigger_task_types: decide native vs custom MT4 feature
map UI fields to MT4 source authority
must_reference_when: Any task involves MT4, MQL4, EAs, terminal behavior, native trading/order/account values, broker execution, WebRequest, DDE, DLLs, Strategy Tester, or the question of whether to build custom vs use native MT4.
DOC_MT4_AUTHORITATIVE_REF.conflict_role: wins for MT4 platform capabilities, limitations, native-source authority, broker variability, and custom-development decision policy.

trigger_task_types: compare sample to canonical bridge executor
must_reference_when: The task specifically mentions this MQL4 file or asks about its contents.
do_not_use_for: Do not treat this file as the canonical EAFIX execution engine; its header says it is a functional replacement example because the original source was unavailable.
DOC_EA_EXECUTION_ENGINE_SAMPLE.conflict_role: lowest authority; never overrides the MT4 authoritative reference, bridge channel catalogs, or canonical module/process contracts.

trigger_task_types: map data source to UI field
must_reference_when: The task concerns any operator-facing UI, dashboard backend, GUI gateway, screen, panel, control, filter, UI-visible field, REST endpoint, WebSocket channel, or safety/disabled-state rule.
DOC_UI_CATALOG.conflict_role: wins for UI structure, UI controls, safety levels, data dependencies, implementation bindings, and UI acceptance criteria.

trigger_task_types: trace CAL8 to SIG8 to execution result
must_reference_when: The task asks for detailed step-by-step lifecycle behavior beyond the compact 26-step aligned process.
DOC_ATOMIC_LIFECYCLE_MD.conflict_role: reference blueprint; when it conflicts with updated_trading_process_aligned.json or process_step_catalog.json, prefer the canonical JSON catalogs for current step IDs and module mapping.

must_reference_when: The task involves CAL8/CAL5/RCI, calendar identity, deterministic calendar decisions, single-writer authority, anti-patterns, or calendar build-phase verification.
DOC_CAL_DES_RULES.conflict_role: wins for deterministic calendar architectural decisions unless explicitly superseded by a newer EAFIX-DES-CAL document.

DOC_ECON_CAL_TECH_ANALYSIS.path: Economic Calendar Subsystem - Technical Analysis & Documentation.pdf
DOC_ECON_CAL_TECH_ANALYSIS.extracted_text_path: pdf_text/Economic Calendar Subsystem - Technical Analysis & Documentation.txt
must_reference_when: The task asks for broad technical understanding of the calendar subsystem or its production architecture.
DOC_ECON_CAL_TECH_ANALYSIS.conflict_role: architecture reference; use DES rules for deterministic decisions and configuration/deployment PDF for deployable config values.

DOC_ECON_CAL_ENHANCEMENT_PLAN.path: Economic Calendar Enhancement Plan - Auto-Download & Critical Gap Fixes.pdf
DOC_ECON_CAL_ENHANCEMENT_PLAN.extracted_text_path: pdf_text/Economic Calendar Enhancement Plan - Auto-Download & Critical Gap Fixes.txt
must_reference_when: The task involves automated calendar acquisition, ForexFactory download, critical gap remediation, or moving from manual calendar files to auto import.
DOC_ECON_CAL_ENHANCEMENT_PLAN.conflict_role: implementation plan; do not override deterministic DES decisions on CAL8/CAL5 identity.

must_reference_when: The task asks for calendar configuration values, deployment configuration, parameter sets, trigger offsets, or enhanced_config.yaml behavior.
DOC_CAL_CONFIG_DEPLOYMENT.conflict_role: wins for configuration examples in this enhanced deployment reference unless live deployment config supersedes it.

DOC_ENHANCED_CAL_IMPL.path: Enhanced Economic Calendar System - Complete Implementation.pdf
DOC_ENHANCED_CAL_IMPL.extracted_text_path: pdf_text/Enhanced Economic Calendar System - Complete Implementation.txt
must_reference_when: The task asks for concrete implementation patterns or class/function-level ideas for the enhanced calendar system.
DOC_ENHANCED_CAL_IMPL.conflict_role: implementation reference only; validate and adapt before production use because it is a large complete-implementation sketch.

must_reference_when: The task asks for build order, dependency layering, or the relationship between CAL8, SQLite, downloader, state machine, proximity, CSV output, MT4 EA, and feedback.
DOC_DEPENDENCY_LAYERS.conflict_role: visual summary only; use DES rules and process catalogs for enforceable details.

subject_routes.subject: MT4 platform behavior, MQL4, Expert Advisors, native terminal capabilities, broker constraints
subject_routes.ai_instruction: Before proposing custom MT4 functionality, check native MT4 capability and source authority. Prefer export/copy/normalize of terminal-owned values over recomputation unless a documented project exception applies.

subject_routes.subject: MT4-Python bridge channels, transports, ports, data contracts, Redis topics, CSV/JSONL/DDE/WebRequest flows
subject_routes.ai_instruction: Resolve protocol, direction, port, data contract, and owning module from communication_channels.json first. Use the text channel analysis for associated file discovery and narrative context.

subject_routes.subject: end-to-end trading lifecycle, process steps, phase contracts, module handoffs, validation/failure gates
subject_routes.ai_instruction: Use process_step_catalog.json for exact step handoffs and updated_trading_process_aligned.json for phase grouping and explicit validation/failure contract IDs.

subject_routes.subject: module definitions, ownership, interfaces, service homes, module groups, file-to-module mapping
subject_routes.ai_instruction: Use module_catalog.json for canonical identity and ownership. Use file_module_mapping.csv only for physical implementation mapping.

subject_routes.subject: economic calendar deterministic architecture, CAL8/CAL5/RCI identity, state machine, proximity, debounce, single-writer calendar policy
subject_routes.ai_instruction: For calendar identity and architecture decisions, apply DES calendar rules first. Then align the decision to process steps/modules and only then use implementation/configuration references.

subject_routes.subject: ForexFactory auto-download, enhanced calendar config, deployment, parameter sets, trigger offsets
subject_routes.ai_instruction: Use the enhancement plan for downloader/gap-fix work and the config/deployment reference for deployable values. Do not override DES identity rules.

subject_routes.subject: operator UI, dashboard backend, GUI gateway, screens, controls, REST/WebSocket contracts, safety controls
subject_routes.ai_instruction: Use ui_catalog.json as the single source of truth for intended UI products, screens, controls, data inputs, safety model, and acceptance criteria.

subject_routes.subject: reentry decision engine, outcome bucketizer, proximity evaluator, matrix lookup, reentry intent builder, matrix UI
subject_routes.ai_instruction: Resolve reentry modules and process steps first, then use calendar DES rules for proximity/identity interactions and UI catalog for operator-facing matrix controls.

subject_routes.subject: risk evaluation, order intent, routing, MT4 transport, broker execution, execution report normalization
subject_routes.ai_instruction: Keep signal/risk/order responsibilities separated. Use MT4 authoritative reference for broker/terminal data and communication channel catalog for transport details.

subject_routes.subject: existing internal services, available capabilities, avoiding duplicate development, cost/use-limit awareness
subject_routes.ai_instruction: Search capability_registry first before recommending custom code. If an internal capability exists, prefer it unless the task requires behavior it cannot provide.
